metadataplusplus v3.0.1 - Passed - Package Tests Results

PUH-BrianMartell Theorem 228 closes the subleading part of NP-6 (PMNS matrix) that T226 left open. It identifies the neutrino-sector phase offset delta_nu = +Q(1-Q)/3 = +2/27 rad as the unique value in the natural candidate space (mirroring T155 v2’s colour CG structure) that reproduces empirical theta_13 = 8.54°. Using the phase-difference relation sin(theta_13) ≈ |delta_lep - delta_nu| gives 4/27 ≈ 0.14815, matching the measured value to 0.24% (predicted theta_13 = 8.52°, well inside the ±0.11° uncertainty). This completes the four-sector phase pattern and explains why the same simple mechanism does not apply to CKM theta_13 (due to strong GST suppression from large quark hierarchies, per T225/T226). The result is clean, unique within the structural candidate space, and maintains honest scope (plausible identification but full E6 × SU(2)_L derivation remains open). NP-6 is now closed at both leading (T226) and subleading levels.
